gloss_en: How are you today? Are you still well?
notes: pan-Peninsular ʕād- 'still' + suffix (watson2002; ingham1994); aw/ay diphthongs retained (kayf, al-yawm) — node conservative rank-1

gloss_en: Today is cold and the sky is clear.
notes: diphthong retained (al-yawm); hamza final samāʔ; ta_marbuta; sun assimilation none; diphthong retention and final hamza per (watson2002)

gloss_en: I don't want coffee, I want tea.
notes: pan-Peninsular abġa 'I want' (ingham1994); ق→q retained is node rank-1 (omar1975 urban Hijazi retention); negation mā

sentence: اِشْتَرَيت ثَلَاثَة أَكْيَاس زَبِيب مِن السُّوق.
raw: اشتريت ثلاثة أكياس زبيب من السوق.
ipa: ʔiʃtaˈrajt θaˈlaːθa ʔakˈjaːs zaˈbiːb ˈmin asˈsuːq
gloss_en: I bought three bags of raisins from the market.
notes: ث retained /θ/ fricative-first is node rank-1 (watson2002); ق→q (sūq); number; diphthong akyās

gloss_en: This man remembers that noon.
notes: ذ retained /ð/ and ظ retained /ðˤ/ fricative-first (watson2002; albalushi2016); geminate جّ; ḏāk demonstrative

sentence: قُوم رُوح لِلسُّوق وَجِيب سَمَك طَازَج.
raw: قوم روح للسوق وجيب سمك طازج.
ipa: ˈquːm ˈruːħ lisˈsuːq waˈdʒiːb ˈsamak ˈtˤɑːzadʒ
gloss_en: Get up, go to the market and get fresh fish.
notes: peninsular imperatives gūm/rūḥ/jīb; ق→q (sūq); ج→dʒ; long vowels; sedentary qāf [q] and ǧīm→[dʒ] per (watson2002)

sentence: الْيَوم مُو حَارّ وَالسَّمَاء فِيهَا غِيُوم.
raw: اليوم مو حار والسماء فيها غيوم.
ipa: alˈjawm ˈmuː ˈħaːrr wassaˈmaːʔ ˈfiːhaː ˈɣiːuːm
gloss_en: Today is not hot and there are clouds in the sky.
notes: negator mū; diphthong retained (al-yawm); hamza samāʔ; geminate رّ; diphthong retention and emphatic/gemination phonology per (watson2002)

gloss_en: How much is the kilo? Five and a half.
notes: bikam question; geminate صّ; number; emphatic ص; Peninsular emphatic/gemination phonology per (watson2002)

gloss_en: The appointment is at half past three in the afternoon.
notes: ث retained /θ/ (watson2002); sun assimilation as-s; emphatic ص; geminate صّ

gloss_en: My elder brother lives in the city.
notes: long vowels; pan-Peninsular axūy; kaf plain /k/; Peninsular vowel phonology per (watson2002)

gloss_en: Go straight ahead and then turn right.
notes: imperative rūḥ; diphthong retained (baʕdayn); geminate فّ; pan-Peninsular sīda 'straight'; diphthong retention per (watson2002)

sentence: الْأَكِل كَان زَين بَسّ الْمَرَق كَان بَارِد.
raw: الأكل كان زين بس المرق كان بارد.
ipa: ˈalʔakil ˈkaːn ˈzajn ˈbass ˈalmaraq ˈkaːn ˈbaːrid
gloss_en: The food was good but the broth was cold.
notes: pan-Peninsular zēn evaluation + maraq 'broth' (ingham1994); geminate سّ; sun assimilation none

gloss_en: Where did you put the key? I did not find it.
notes: pan-Peninsular wēn (ingham1994); diphthong environment; geminate طّ; negation mā

gloss_en: How many came yesterday?
notes: ج→dʒ; hamza in jāʔ; kam + number question; pan-Peninsular ams 'yesterday'; ǧīm→[dʒ] and final hamza per (watson2002)

gloss_en: If you come early we go together.
notes: conditional iḏa with retained interdental ذ (watson2002); ج→dʒ; long vowels

sentence: رِحِت أَمْس لِلسُّوق وَجِبِت سَمَك طَازَج.
raw: رحت أمس للسوق وجبت سمك طازج.
ipa: ˈriħit ˈʔams lisˈsuːq ˈwadʒibit ˈsamak ˈtˤɑːzadʒ
gloss_en: I went to the market yesterday and got fresh fish.
notes: past narrative; ق→q (sūq); ج→dʒ; pan-Peninsular ams; diphthong absent; sedentary qāf [q] and ǧīm→[dʒ] per (watson2002)

gloss_en: Tomorrow we travel early in the morning.
notes: pan-Peninsular bākir 'tomorrow' (ingham1994); sun assimilation aṣ-ṣ; kaf plain; emphatic ص

gloss_en: Hello, who is with me?
notes: phone frame; pan-Peninsular minu 'who' (ingham1994); long vowel

gloss_en: Good morning, how are you?
notes: greeting pair; kayf ḥālak; diphthong retained in al-xayr, kayf (watson2002)

gloss_en: Patience is good, may you live long.
notes: proverb closing; pan-Peninsular zēn; ṭawīl al-ʕumr politeness; geminate صّ; emphatic ص; Peninsular emphatic/gemination phonology per (watson2002)
